items.headline: RBI maintains repo rate, revises GDP growth forecast downward and inflation upward for FY27
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) maintained its repo rate at 5.25% with a neutral stance. It revised India's GDP growth forecast for FY27 downward from 6.9% to 6.6% and increased its CPI inflation projection to 5.1% from 4.6% for FY27, citing upside risks. India's retail inflation (CPI) rose to 3.93% in May 2026. Prabhudas Lilladher anticipates inflation exceeding 6% in H2 FY27. RBI also announced a two-day Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auction of ₹50,000 crore.

items.headline: Government and RBI to implement further measures to attract foreign capital
items.summary: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that the government and RBI would implement further measures to attract foreign capital. This follows earlier reforms to deepen the Government Securities market and increase Foreign Portfolio Investor (FPI) participation. The RBI also allowed public sector undertakings and banks to raise funds from overseas markets with hedging costs borne by the central bank.

items.headline: Indian markets mixed awaiting US Fed decision; crude falls on easing geopolitical tensions
items.summary: Indian markets are exhibiting mixed trends as investors await the US Federal Reserve's rate decision, widely anticipated to maintain rates at 3.5-3.75%. Positive sentiment stems from easing geopolitical tensions, including a proposed US-Iran peace agreement, leading to Brent crude falling below $80 per barrel. US markets were mixed overnight, with Asian markets opening cautiously.

items.headline: Ansal Properties, Resourceful Automobile, and Q-line Biotech scheduled to announce results today
items.summary: Ansal Properties & Infrastructure Ltd, Resourceful Automobile, and Q-line Biotech are scheduled to announce their audited results and/or final dividends today, June 17, 2026. Other companies like Binny, CMX Holdings, Hindustan Adhesives, and Shree Ram Proteins have upcoming results later this month.

items.headline: Realty, IT, Media, Consumer Durables, and FMCG sectors show mixed performance
items.summary: On June 16, Realty, Information Technology, Media, Consumer Durables, and FMCG were top-performing sectors, while Metals underperformed. As of June 17, Nifty IT and Nifty Consumer Durables are performing well, with Nifty Metal and Nifty Realty among the underperformers. Fertilizers, Aquaculture, and Waste Management saw significant gains on Tuesday, while Aluminium declined.

items.summary: NTPC proposes to raise up to Rs 18,000 crore through non-convertible debentures.

items.summary: Bank of Maharashtra revised its MCLR rates, effective today.

items.headline: GIC Re government OFS oversubscribed 3.72 times by institutional investors
items.summary: General Insurance Corporation of India (GIC Re) saw its government Offer For Sale (OFS) oversubscribed 3.72 times by institutional investors. Retail investors can bid today.

items.headline: KIMS launches QIP with a floor price of Rs 771.73/share
items.summary: KIMS launched a Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP) with a floor price of Rs 771.73/share. The issue opened on June 16, and the final price will be determined on June 19.
